page: Amazon Web Services t3a.xlarge is a burstable virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 vCPUs, which map to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 16.0 GB of DDR4 system memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the t3a.xlarge sits in the lower performance tiers for sustained workloads. Both single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng and Geekbench place the instance in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show weak to poor performance, with large-block write speeds falling into the bottom 10% and read speeds in the bottom 25%. However, the processor demonstrates average performance in the Passmark CPU encryption test, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI instruction sets.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t3a.xlarge offers a budget-friendly profile for workloads with variable CPU utilization. Because it uses a burstable CPU allocation model, it is highly resource-dense and cost-effective for idle or low-utilization scenarios, but it presents a performance tradeoff under sustained compute loads where CPU credits may be depleted. The lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy operations.

This instance type is best suited for low-traffic web serving, small database operations, development or staging environments, and light background scripting. It is not recommended for high-throughput database systems, intensive LLM inference, or sustained high-performance computing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services t3a.xlarge is an x86_64 burstable instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz. It features 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators bundled. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance is designed for budget-conscious workloads with variable CPU demands. Benchmark results indicate generally weak to poor performance across compute and memory bandwidth tests, though it achieves average results in encryption tasks due to hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI support. The burstable CPU allocation model makes it highly cost-effective for non-sustained workloads but introduces performance tradeoffs under continuous load. It is best suited for general-purpose, low-utilization tasks such as development environments, light background processing, and low-traffic web servers.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3a.xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7571 processor, designed for budget-friendly, variable workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t3a.xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor for low-utilization workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly burstable instance with balanced memory and compute resources, designed for variable workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.2 GHz AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB DDR4 RAM
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model suited for variable or spikey workloads
Features hardware-accelerated encryption via AES and SHA_NI instruction sets
Operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Lacks bundled local storage and dedicated GPU accelerators
Best suited for development environments, light background tasks, and low-traffic web serving
